title: As a Canadian M.Sc. student of HRM, I have an assignment and i want you to give me a very detailed structure to get this…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
As a Canadian M.Sc. student of HRM, I have an assignment and i want you to give me a very detailed structure to get this done.
The brief goes thus:

Module learning outcomes assessed by this task:
1. MO1: Understand the nature of contemporary people management in its broader context (including product markets, business strategy, labour markets, organisations, occupational structures, industry sector and national business systems)
2. MO3: Critically assess core concepts and contemporary practice in HRM, including reward and performance management, employee involvement and resourcing, and the role of tech in the delivery of HRM.
3. MO5: Understand critical HRM concerns, including ethical decision-making and managing diversity

Completing your assessment 
This is an individual written assignment which requires students to write a 1,500 word literature review based on one of the topics raised in the final consultancy case study. 

What am I required to do on this assessment?
 Evidence understanding of the topic and key theories
 Demonstrates critical appreciation of relevant theory and empirical research
 Draw from relevant, academic sources to work
 Assignment is written in accordance with traditional academic requirements
 Sources should be cited and referenced using the Harvard system

What do I need to do to pass? 
The following criteria are considered when marking:
1. Literature coverage and subject knowledge
2. Critical evaluation
3. Narrative and structure
4. Use of literature and other sources
5. Writing quality

How do I achieve high marks in this assessment? 
Demonstrate the following:
1. Original and innovative use of an impressive range of sources, many beyond those recommended for the topic. Outstanding coverage of all key aspects of the topics literature. Excellent theoretical and conceptual grounding and ability to contextualise key theories and debates within the literature.
2. Outstanding attempt present, critique and contextualise the literature by making judgements about information, validity of ideas and theories or quality of research on a set of theoretically-based criteria. Outstanding links are made between theory 
and relevant examples and practice.
3. Clear and holistic evaluation of the current literature on the topic. Outstanding level of depth and sophistication. Excellent structure which helps develop detailed and comprehensive understanding of all key debates and the themes which underly 
them. No irrelevant material. Evidence of independent, insightful, innovative thought. 
4. Outstanding use of external sources, referencing that conforms precisely to UWE Harvard requirements.
5. Highest standards of literacy (i.e. academic vocabulary, spelling) and presentation. Outstanding clarity, structure and organisation (including paragraph structure, introduction and conclusion). Excellent logical flow throughout. Direction of 
argument is clearly signposted throughout.

Marking Guide is as follows
LITERATURE COVERAGE AND SUBJECT KNOWLEDGE 20% - Original / innovative use of animpressive range of sources, many beyond those recommended for the topic. Outstanding coverage of all key aspects of the topics literature. Excellent theoretical / conceptual grounding and ability to contextualise key theories/debates within the literature. (18-20 marks)
CRITICAL EVALUATION 30% - Outstanding attempt present, critique and contextualise the literature by making judgements about information, validity ofideas/theory or qualityof research on a set of theoretically-based criteria. Outstanding links are made between theory and relevant examples /practice. (27-30 marks)
NARRATIVE AND STRUCTURE 25% - Clear and holistic evaluation of the current literature on the topic. Outstanding level of depth and sophistication. Excellent structure which helps develop detailed and comprehensive understanding of all key debates and the themes which underly them. No irrelevant material. Evidence of independent, insightful, innovative thought. (23-25 marks)
USE OF LITERATURE AND OTHER SOURCES 10% - Outstanding use of external sources, referencing that conforms precisely to UWE Harvard requirements. (9-10 marks)
WRITING QUALITY 15% - Highest standards of literacy (i.e. academic vocabulary, spelling) and presentation. Outstanding clarity, structure and organisation (including paragraph structure, introduction and conclusion). Excellent logical flow throughout. 
Direction of argument is clearly signposted throughout. (13-15 marks)

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
To complete this assignment, you will need to follow the following steps:

Step 1: Choose a topic from the final consultancy case study 

Before you start your literature review, you will need to choose a topic from the final consultancy case study provided for the assignment. Consider your interests, the module learning outcomes, and the amount of literature available on the topic you choose.

Step 2: Conduct research and understand the topic and key theories 

Once you have chosen a topic, you will need to conduct research and gather information about the topic. Search for relevant academic sources, including academic journals, books, and credible websites. Also, look for any key theories and concepts related to your topic.

Step 3: Write a literature review 

The literature review should be 1,500 words long and should include an introduction, body, and conclusion. The body of the literature review should include an evaluation of the key theories and concepts related to your chosen topic. You should also include an in-depth analysis of the current literature on the topic, making judgements about the validity and quality of the information based on a set of theoretically-based criteria.

Step 4: Follow traditional academic requirements 

When writing your literature review, it is important to follow traditional academic requirements. Your assignment should be written in a formal, academic style and should conform to the UWE Harvard referencing system. You should also structure your assignment in a logical and organized manner.

Step 5: Edit and proofread your work 

Before submitting your assignment, be sure to edit and proofread your work thoroughly. Check for grammatical errors, spelling mistakes, and ensure that your writing is clear and concise. Use academic vocabulary appropriately.

To achieve high marks in this assignment, you should strive to:

- Demonstrate original and innovative use of sources, beyond what is recommended for the topic
- Show excellent theoretical and conceptual grounding in the key theories and debates related to your topic
- Critique and contextualize the literature by making judgements about the validity and quality of research on a set of theoretically-based criteria
- Use outstanding external sources and conform precisely to UWE Harvard referencing requirements
- Follow the highest standards of literacy and presentation, including logical structure and a clear, direction of argument throughout.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Give me a comprehensive and detailed structure to get this done

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Sure! Here's a comprehensive and detailed structure for completing the literature review assignment:

I. Introduction
- Briefly introduce the topic of your literature review
- Provide some context on the importance of this topic in contemporary people management
- Highlight the aims and objectives of your literature review

II. Current practices in contemporary people management
- Discuss the broader context of contemporary people management, including product markets, business strategy, labor markets, organizations, occupational structures, industry sectors, and national business systems
- Analyze key trends, issues, and challenges in contemporary people management
- Evaluate the impact of technology and globalization on contemporary people management

III. Core concepts and contemporary practices in HRM
- Critically assess core concepts and contemporary practices in HRM, including reward and performance management, employee involvement and resourcing, and the role of technology in the delivery of HRM
- Analyze recent developments and emerging issues in these areas
- Assess the effectiveness and limitations of current HRM practices in addressing contemporary people management challenges

IV. Ethical decision-making and managing diversity in HRM
- Understand critical HRM concerns, including ethical decision-making and managing diversity
- Discuss key ethical issues and dilemmas faced by HR professionals in today's diverse workplaces
- Evaluate the effectiveness of ethical codes, guidelines, and frameworks in guiding ethical decision-making in HRM
- Analyze best practices in managing diversity in the workplace, including recruiting, training, mentoring, and supporting diverse employees

V. Critique and synthesis of the literature
- Synthesize the literature, develop a coherent argument and comprehensive understanding of all key debates
- Provide critical insights into the topic, drawing connections between theory and practice
- Identify gaps and opportunities for future research
- Develop recommendations for practitioners and policymakers

VI. Conclusion
- Summarize the main findings of your literature review
- Reiterate the importance of the topic in contemporary people management
- Highlight the implications of your analysis for HR professionals, policymakers, and scholars

VII. References
- Provide a complete list of references cited in your literature review, formatted according to the UWE Harvard style

That should give you a good structure to work with. Make sure that each section is well-written, clearly organized, and backed up with relevant sources. Good luck!
